# servicenow_certificate (Data Source)

`servicenow_certificate` data source can be used to retrieve information of a single certificate record in ServiceNow by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing certificate record in ServiceNow by name.
data "servicenow_certificate" "example" {
  name = "example-trust-cert"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the certificate record.

### Read-Only

- `active` (Boolean) If set to 'true', the certificate is active.
- `expiration` (String) Expiration date of the certificate. Typically computed by ServiceNow from the certificate body.
- `format` (String) Format of the certificate. Allowed values are pem, der, jks or pfx.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `issuer` (String) Issuer DN parsed from the certificate.
- `key_store` (String, Sensitive) Base64-encoded keystore content (used when format is jks or pfx). Sensitive: the value is not read back from ServiceNow.
- `key_store_password` (String, Sensitive) Password used to unlock the keystore. Sensitive: the value is not read back from ServiceNow.
- `pem_certificate` (String, Sensitive) PEM encoded certificate body. Sensitive: the value is not read back from ServiceNow.
- `short_description` (String) Short description of the certificate record.
- `subject` (String) Subject DN parsed from the certificate.
- `type` (String) Type of the certificate. Allowed values are trust_store, java_key_store or key_pair.
- `valid_from` (String) Date from which the certificate is valid. Typically computed by ServiceNow from the certificate body.
